title: Lesson 15 — Poison and Cure

words.example: The deleterious effects of too little sleep add up fast.
Noxious fumes forced an evacuation of the building.

words.definition: extremely severe or harmful; (of disease) spreading quickly
words.example: A virulent strain of the flu swept through the dorms.

words.definition: proceeding in a gradual, subtle way but with harmful effects
words.example: The insidious spread of the rot went unnoticed for months.
The baneful influence of the gang hung over the block.
Skipping meals is detrimental to concentration.
The salubrious mountain air did the patients good.

words.definition: gentle and harmless; (of a tumor) not malignant
words.example: The growth was benign, the doctor reassured her.
An innocuous remark that somehow started a feud.
A benevolent employer who quietly paid for tuition.

words.definition: a person who gives money or other help to a cause or person
words.example: An anonymous benefactor funded the whole library.

words.definition: a supposed remedy or solution for all problems
words.example: New technology is no panacea for bad management.

story: The old chemical plant had a [[deleterious|deleterious]] effect on the valley: [[noxious|noxious]] smoke, a [[virulent|virulent]] outbreak of coughs, and an [[insidious|insidious]] contamination that seeped into the wells for years. Locals called its [[baneful|baneful]] presence the most [[detrimental|detrimental]] thing ever to happen to the town. Then things turned. Cleaner air proved [[salubrious|salubrious]]; the once-feared river ran [[benign|benign]] and [[innocuous|innocuous]] again. A [[benevolent|benevolent]] [[benefactor|benefactor]] funded the cleanup, though even she warned that money alone was no [[panacea|panacea]] for decades of neglect.
